title: At The Mid Hour Of Night
content:
At the mid hour of night, when stars are weeping, I fly
    To the lone vale we loved, when life shone warm in thine eye;
    And I think oft, if spirits can steal from the regions of air,
    To revisit past scenes of delight, thou wilt come to me there,
    And tell me our love is remembered, even in the sky.

    Then I sing the wild song 'twas once such pleasure to hear
    When our voices commingling breathed, like one, on the ear;
    And, as Echo far off thro' the vale my sad orison rolls,
    I think, oh my love! 'tis thy voice from the Kingdom of Souls,[1]
    Faintly answering still the notes that once were so dear.
meaning:
Thomas Moore's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "At The Mid Hour Of Night"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
